text: Unit tests check individual pieces of code, like functions or methods, to ensure they work correctly in isolation. Integration tests verify that different parts of a system function together as expected. While unit tests are quick and focused, integration tests are broader and can catch issues between components. Test-driven development, or TDD, involves writing tests before writing code. This approach encourages clearer design and ensures new features are testable from the start. Together, these testing methods build confidence in the software. When developers know tests cover both small units and larger interactions, they can make changes more safely and release updates with greater peace of mind.
